rationale: Ground truth is sparse (two LGTM approvals, one thread about a change later removed from the PR), so neither candidate has much to recall, but both independently converge on the same underlying theme dsocek raised — stop hardcoding component lists and derive from an existing single source (both cite registry.py:427 and _dit_modules/SupportsComponentDiscovery, a good sign of grounding since they agree). Y is more rigorous: every claim carries explicit grep/read evidence, it hits the 'derive from existing source' theme twice with concrete refactor proposals, and it surfaces additional verifiably-affected pipelines (ovis_image, flux2_klein) that X and the human reviewers missed. X is tighter and also well-grounded (correct scope analysis, text-encoder gap) but with fewer, less evidenced findings. Neither has a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
